title: glochidion collinum
intro: Glochidion collinum, also known as the climbing milk bush, is a species of plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Glochidion collinum include reducing fever, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and alleviating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
